title: Charred Mexican Chicken Mole Poblano
description: A bold, aerated dish of charred chicken smothered in smoky mole sauce. It finishes with a bright lime vinaigrette for a refreshing contrast.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425°F and cut the chicken thighs into 1-inch pieces, seasoning with salt, pepper, chipotle, ancho, cocoa, and cumin.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat, then add the seasoned chicken and sear until the edges are charred, about 4 minutes.
Transfer the skillet to the oven and roast the chicken until cooked through, about 10 minutes, flipping halfway.
While the chicken cooks, combine the confitted tomato, red onion, and broth in the same skillet, whisking to create a sauce.
Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer and let it reduce, stirring occasionally, until slightly thickened, 8 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and pour the reduced sauce over it, letting it soak for 3 minutes.
Finish the mole by stirring in a squeeze of lime juice, a drizzle of olive oil, and fresh chopped cilantro.
Serve the charred chicken with the aerated mole sauce drizzled over a bed of quinoa or a side of corn tortillas.
